column2: A laptop and access point from different vendors can still connect because both follow the same Wi-Fi standards family. Which IEEE standards family is this?
column5:
Why

802.11 is the IEEE wireless networking family used for Wi-Fi. Standards let different vendors build compatible wireless devices.

Exam Trap

Do not confuse the standards family with a single vendor feature or a router model.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.8; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.2
column10: A+::220-1201::2.2 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::WirelessNetworkTechnologies Basic HighYield Scenario WiFi IEEE Source::Messer-v170

column2: Several nearby Wi-Fi networks use overlapping frequency groups. What channel-planning goal should improve coexistence when possible?
column5:
Why

Channels are groups of frequencies. When nearby networks avoid overlap, they are less likely to compete with each other on the same radio space.

Exam Trap

Do not jump to replacing hardware before checking basic wireless planning choices.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.8; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.2
column10: A+::220-1201::2.2 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::WirelessNetworkTechnologies Basic HighYield Scenario WiFi Channels Source::Messer-v170

column2: A user wants a short-range wireless headset without joining the office Wi-Fi network. Which wireless technology is the best match?
column5:
Why

Bluetooth is commonly used to remove cables from nearby peripherals such as headsets, speakers, keyboards, and mice.

Exam Trap

Bluetooth personal-area connections are not the same as Wi-Fi infrastructure networking.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.8; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.2
column10: A+::220-1201::2.2 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::WirelessNetworkTechnologies Basic HighYield Scenario Bluetooth Peripherals Source::Messer-v170

column2: A warehouse wants tags that can identify inventory items when scanned by radio. Which wireless technology fits that use case?
column5:
Why

RFID is commonly used for identification and tracking, including inventory, access badges, and similar tagged items.

Source Note

This card tests the use case, not a specific vendor's tag format.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.8; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.2
column10: A+::220-1201::2.2 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::WirelessNetworkTechnologies Basic Scenario RFID Tracking Source::Messer-v170

column2: A phone completes a payment by being held very close to a payment terminal. Which short-range wireless technology is being used?
column5:
Why

NFC supports short-range two-way communication and is commonly associated with payment systems and access-token style interactions.

Exam Trap

Do not choose Bluetooth just because the device is wireless. The close tap-style interaction points to NFC.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.8; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.2
column10: A+::220-1201::2.2 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::WirelessNetworkTechnologies Basic HighYield Scenario NFC Payments Source::Messer-v170

column2: Which technology is the better match for a short-range two-way tap interaction: RFID-style inventory tracking or NFC?
column5:
Why

NFC builds on RFID concepts but is associated with very short-range two-way interactions such as payment and access-token workflows.

Exam Trap

RFID is often used for identification and tracking; NFC is the stronger match when the scenario emphasizes a close two-way tap interaction.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.8; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.2
column10: A+::220-1201::2.2 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::WirelessNetworkTechnologies Basic Comparison NFC RFID Source::Messer-v170

column2: A device uses a brief tap to help start pairing with another wireless technology. Which technology can help bootstrap Bluetooth pairing?
column5:
Why

The approved source identifies NFC as a possible bootstrap method for another wireless connection such as Bluetooth pairing.

Exam Trap

The tap does not mean the entire connection stays on NFC. NFC can assist setup for a different wireless link.
